name: 人类遗传病

本模块选取的减数分裂和受精作用、DNA分子的结构和功能、遗传和变异的基本原理及应用等知识，主要是从细胞水平和分子水平阐述生命的延续性。

analysis to study the inheritance pattern of human genetic diseases (Figure 12.6). Chapter 12 | Mendel's Experiments and Heredity
Figure 12.6 Alkaptonuria is a recessive genetic disorder in which two amino acids, phenylalanine and tyrosine, are not properly metabolized. Affected individuals may have darkened skin and brown urine, and may suffer joint damage and other complications. In this pedigree, individuals with the disorder are indicated in blue and have the genotype aa. Unaffected individuals are indicated in yellow and have the genotype AA or Aa. Note that it is often possible to determine a person’s genotype from the genotype of their offspring. For example, if neither parent has the disorder but their child does, they must be heterozygous. Two individuals on the pedigree have an unaffected phenotype but unknown genotype. Because they do not have the disorder, they must have at least one normal allele, so their genotype gets the “A?” designation.

This image appears on page 1455 of the textbook. ![Figure 1154 - A large illustration or diagram (545×742px) related to: FIGU](OpenStax_Biology_2e_images/img_1154_d53c6639.jpg) 47.4 Preserving Biodiversity ## Learning Objectives

Habitat destruction can affect ecosystems other than forests. Rivers and streams are important ecosystems that are frequently modified through land development, damming, channelizing, or water removal. Damming affects the water flow to all parts of a river, which can reduce or eliminate populations that had adapted to the natural flow of the river. For example, an estimated 91 percent of United States rivers have been altered in some way. Modifications include dams, to create energy or store water; levees, to prevent flooding; and dredging or rerouting, to create land that is more suitable for human development. Many fish and amphibian species and numerous freshwater clams in the United States have seen declines caused by river damming and habitat loss. Habitat destruction, even when undertaken on behalf of humans, can lead to negative effects for us as well. Excessive soil erosion after forest removal, for example, can remove fertile soil and make river water toxic. Scientists and conservationists such as Wangari Maathai, who founded the Green Belt movement in Kenya, focus on replanting trees to repair habitats and prevent damage from deforestation. Maathai was awarded a Nobel Prize for her work, but unfortunately passed away in 2011. Overharvesting Overharvesting is a serious threat to many species, but particularly to aquatic (both marine and freshwater) species. Despite regulation and monitoring, there are recent examples of fishery collapse. The western Atlantic cod fishery is the among the most significant. While it was a hugely productive fishery for 400 years, the i

supplements.curriculum_md_raw:
# 人类遗传病

## 基本信息
- **学科**: 生物
- **学段**: 高中
- **年级**: 高中11年级
- **章节**: 遗传的基本规律
- **知识点**: 人类遗传病
- **课标版本**: 普通高中课程标准（2017年版2020年修订）

---

## 一、课标原文

### 1.1 内容要求
> （生物 11年级）人类遗传病的内容要求。学生应理解人类遗传病的基本概念，掌握基本方法，能运用所学知识解决简单的实际问题。

### 1.2 学业要求
> 学生能准确描述人类遗传病的相关概念，能进行基本的运算和推理，能解决与人类遗传病相关的实际问题，形成初步的生物思维和素养。

### 1.3 教学提示
> 教学时应注重从学生熟悉的生活情境入手，引导学生通过具体操作、观察、归纳等方式理解人类遗传病的概念。注重知识的发生发展过程，帮助学生建立知识之间的联系。

---

## 二、教学目标

### 2.1 知识与技能
- 学生能够理解人类遗传病的基本概念和原理
- 学生能够正确进行相关计算和推理
- 学生能够识别和描述相关的概念、现象或材料
- 学生能够理解人类遗传病的基本概念和原理

### 2.2 过程与方法
- 学生经历通过自主探究和合作交流，掌握学习生物学的方法
- 学生能够运用生物学学科方法解决问题
- 学生能够在交流中表达自己的思路
- 学生经历通过自主探究和合作交流，掌握学习生物学的方法

### 2.3 情感态度与价值观
- 学生感受生物学与现实生活密切相关
- 学生体验生物学探究的乐趣，树立学好生物学的信心
- 学生养成认真细致、独立思考的学习习惯
- 学生感受生物学与现实生活密切相关

---

## 三、教学重难点

### 3.1 教学重点
- 人类遗传病的基本内容和基本方法
- 形成完整的知识体系结构
- 人类遗传病的基本内容和基本方法

- 建立知识之间的联系，实现知识的迁移应用

supplements.textbook_summary:
## 教材化讲解参考（由课标要点生成）

### 核心概念
人类遗传病是生物学学习中的重要知识点。教学时应围绕课标要点“本模块包括遗传的细胞基础、遗传的分子基础、遗传的基本规律、生物的变异和生物的进化等内容。”展开，先明确概念边界，再通过例子、图示、实验、语篇或案例帮助学生建立可迁移的理解。

### 学习路径
1. 情境导入：以结构与功能、生命过程或生态关系为主线组织「人类遗传病」教学。
2. 探究建构：通过图示、模型、实验或真实案例，引导学生解释生命现象背后的机制。
3. 应用迁移：围绕课标要点“本模块包括遗传的细胞基础、遗传的分子基础、遗传的基本规律、生物的变异和生物的进化等内容。”，设计观察记录、证据解释和生活应用任务。

### 例题设计方向
可设计一道“识别核心概念”的基础题，一道“解释现象或文本材料”的理解题，一道“迁移到真实情境”的应用题，并要求学生说明依据。

Figure 12.6 Alkaptonuria is a recessive genetic disorder in which two amino acids, phenylalanine and tyrosine, are not properly metabolized. Affected individuals may have darkened skin and brown urine, and may suffer joint damage and other complications. In this pedigree, individuals with the disorder are indicated in blue and have the genotype aa. Unaffected individuals are indicated in yellow and have the genotype AA or Aa. Note that it is often possible to determine a person’s genotype from the genotype of their offspring. For example, if neither parent has the disorder but their child does, they must be heterozygous. Because they do not have the disorder, they must have at least one normal allele, so their genotype gets the “A?” designation.

This image appears on page 1455 of the textbook. ![Figure 1154 - A large illustration or diagram (545×742px) related to: FIGU](OpenStax_Biology_2e_images/img_1154_d53c6639.jpg) 47.4 Preserving Biodiversity ## Learning Objectives

Habitat destruction can affect ecosystems other than forests. Rivers and streams are important ecosystems that are frequently modified through land development, damming, channelizing, or water removal. Damming affects the water flow to all parts of a river, which can reduce or eliminate populations that had adapted to the natural flow of the river. For example, an estimated 91 percent of United States rivers have been altered in some way. Modifications include dams, to create energy or store water; levees, to prevent flooding; and dredging or rerouting, to create land that is more suitable for human development. Many fish and amphibian species and numerous freshwater clams in the United States have seen declines caused by river damming and habitat loss. Habitat destruction, even when undertaken on behalf of humans, can lead to negative effects for us as well. Excessive soil erosion after forest removal, for example, can remove fertile soil and make river water toxic. Scientists and conservationists such as Wangari Maathai, who founded the Green Belt movement in Kenya, focus on replanting trees to repair habitats and prevent damage from deforestation. Maathai was awarded a Nobel Prize for her work, but unfortunately passed away in 2011. Overharvesting Overharvesting is a serious threat to many species, but particularly to aquatic (both marine and freshwater) species. Despite regulation and monitoring, there are recent examples of fishery collapse. The western Atlantic cod fishery is the among the most significant. While it was a hugely productive fishery for 400 years, the i

b. 10,000 c. 100,000 d. 1,000,000 6. An adaptive radiation is________. a. a burst of speciation b. a healthy level of UV radiation c. a hypothesized cause of a mass extinction d. evidence of an asteroid impact 7. The number of currently described species on the planet is about ________. a. 17,000 b. 150,000 c. 1.5 million d. 10 million 8. A mass extinction is defined as ________. a. a loss of 95 percent of species b. an asteroid impact c. a boundary between geological periods d. a loss of 50 percent of species 9. A secondary plant compound might be used for which of the following? a. a new crop variety b. a new drug c. a soil nutrient d. a pest of a crop pest 10. Pollination is an example of ________. a. a possible source of new drugs b. chemical diversity c. an ecosystem service d. crop pest control 11. What is an ecosystem service that performs the same function as a pesticide? a. pollination b. secondary plant compounds c. crop diversity d. predators of pests 12. Which two extinction risks may be a direct result of the pet trade? a. climate change and exotic species introduction b. habitat loss and overharvesting c. overharvesting and exotic species introduction d. habitat loss and climate change 13. Exotic species are especially threatening to what kind of ecosystem? a. deserts b. marine ecosystems c. islands d. tropical forests 14. Certain parrot species cannot be brought to the United States to be sold as pets. What is the name of the legislation that makes this illegal? a. Red List b. Migratory Bird Act c.

exercises.stem: 依据课标「人类遗传病」：本模块包括遗传的细胞基础、遗传的分子基础、遗传的基本规律、生物的变异和生物的进化等内容。 请简要阐述并举例。

exercises.stem: 比较说明：本模块选取的减数分裂和受精作用、DNA分子的结构和功能、遗传和变异的基本原理及应用等知识，主要是从细胞水平和分子水平阐述生命的延续性。
exercises.answer: 需写出关键概念、过程或方法，避免只背结论。

real_world: 观察人体健康、植物生长、生态环境或食品生产中与「人类遗传病」相关的生命现象。
